issuesFound.title: HTML element missing lang attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The <html> element does not have a lang attribute. Screen readers rely on this to announce the page language correctly. Without it, users may hear content read with the wrong accent or pronunciation rules.

issuesFound.title: Page title is empty
issuesFound.explanation: The <title> element is empty. Browser tabs and screen readers use this to identify the page. An empty title makes navigation difficult for users relying on assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 8: <title>Luminary Studio — Accessible design and productivity tools</title>

issuesFound.title: Navigation landmark missing distinguishing label
issuesFound.explanation: There are multiple <nav> elements on the page. This instance lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby, making it impossible for screen reader users to distinguish it from other navigation regions.

issuesFound.title: Button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The button contains only an icon with aria-hidden="true" and no text or aria-label. Screen reader users will hear "button" with no context. It also functions as a toggle but lacks aria-expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 40: <button type="button" class="nav-toggle" aria-label="Toggle navigation menu" aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="nav-menu">

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: This button controls the visibility of the "solutions-submenu" but lacks aria-expanded to indicate its current state. Screen reader users cannot tell if the menu is open or closed.

issuesFound.title: Section landmark missing distinguishing label
issuesFound.explanation: There are multiple <section> elements on the page. This hero section lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby, making it difficult for users to identify this region among others.

issuesFound.title: Broken ARIA reference
issuesFound.explanation: The section references aria-labelledby="features-heading", but no element with id="features-heading" exists in the document. Screen readers will not find the label for this region.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 158:

Everything you need to ship accessible software

issuesFound.title: Image missing alt attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The image at /img/hero-dashboard.png lacks an alt attribute. Screen reader users will not know what information this image conveys.
issuesFound.fix: Line 151:

issuesFound.title: Link text is not descriptive
issuesFound.explanation: The link text "Learn more" does not describe the destination without surrounding context. Screen reader users navigating by links will not know what they are learning more about.
issuesFound.fix: Line 188: Learn more about the scanner

issuesFound.title: Heading level skip
issuesFound.explanation: The heading hierarchy jumps from h2 (Line 197) to h5 (Line 202), skipping h3 and h4. This breaks the logical outline structure for assistive technology users.

issuesFound.title: Form input missing accessible label
issuesFound.explanation: The email input has no associated <label>, aria-label, or aria-labelledby. Screen reader users will not know what information to enter in this field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 633: <input type="email" id="newsletter-email" name="email" autocomplete="email" required aria-required="true" placeholder="you@example.com" aria-label="Email address for newsletter">

issuesFound.title: Personal data input missing autocomplete
issuesFound.explanation: The first name input collects personal data but lacks an autocomplete attribute. This prevents browsers from helping users fill in the form correctly.
issuesFound.fix: Line 666: <input type="text" id="contact-first-name" name="first_name" autocomplete="given-name" required>

issuesFound.title: Form input missing accessible label and autocomplete
issuesFound.explanation: The email input has no associated <label> or aria-label, and lacks autocomplete. Screen reader users won't know the purpose, and browsers can't assist with filling it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 683: <input type="email" id="contact-email" name="email" autocomplete="email" required aria-required="true" aria-label="Email address">

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px but provides no visible border, box-shadow, or non-zero outline. Keyboard users relying on focus rings will not see where focus is located.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside @media (forced-colors: active), :focus-visible sets outline: none without a visible replacement. This removes the focus indicator entirely for users in High Contrast Mode.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector removes the outline and relies only on outline-offset, which is invisible without a non-zero outline width. Keyboard users cannot identify focused buttons.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The logo link's focus state removes the outline without providing a border or box-shadow. Keyboard users cannot detect focus on the home link.
issuesFound.fix: Line 583: .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button removes its outline on focus without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see when this control is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu links remove the outline on focus without a border or shadow replacement. Keyboard users navigating submenus will lose track of focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 658: [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: While #search-input:focus has a box-shadow, the specific #search-input:focus-visible selector removes the outline without a visible alternative. Keyboard-only focus is not indicated.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button removes its outline on focus without a visible border or shadow. Keyboard users cannot identify the button focus state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 709: #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove the outline on focus without replacement. Keyboard users cannot track focus in the account menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 733: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Feature card links remove the outline on focus without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot identify the focused link within the card.
issuesFound.fix: Line 965: .feature-card a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons remove the outline on focus without replacement. Keyboard users cannot see which filter is currently focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1085: .filter-tabs button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons remove the outline on focus without replacement. Keyboard users cannot identify the focused billing option.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1175: .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .table-wrapper:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollable table wrapper removes the outline on focus without replacement. Keyboard users cannot tell when the scrollable region is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion buttons remove the outline on focus without replacement. Keyboard users cannot identify the focused question.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1365: .faq-item dt button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Form inputs remove the outline on focus-visible without a visible alternative (box-shadow is only on :focus). Keyboard focus is not indicated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1438: .form-group input:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Checkboxes remove the outline on focus-visible without replacement. Keyboard users cannot see which checkbox is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1455: .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .footer-brand a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer brand links remove the outline on focus without replacement. Keyboard users cannot track focus in the footer.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1495: .footer-brand a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links remove the outline on focus without replacement. Keyboard users cannot identify focused links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1505: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .social-links a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links remove the outline on focus without replacement. Keyboard users cannot see focus on social icons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1515: .social-links a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn min-height 32px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets min-height: 32px, which is below the recommended 44px touch target size (WCAG 2.5.8). Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate buttons.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the minimum 24px requirement. Small buttons are difficult to tap accurately.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-lg min-height 36px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-lg class sets min-height: 36px, which is below the recommended 44px touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon min-width/min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width and min-height to 20px, below the 24px minimum. Icon buttons are too small for reliable touch interaction.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .nav-toggle min-height/min-width 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle button sets min-height and min-width to 28px, below the recommended 44px size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #nav-menu > li > a min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links have a min-height of 28px, below the recommended 44px touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: [id$="-submenu"] li a min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Submenu links have a min-height of 28px, below the recommended 44px touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #search-input height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search input has a height of 28px, below the recommended 44px touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"] width/height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is 28x28px, below the recommended 44px touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .pricing-toggle button min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons have a min-height of 28px, below the recommended 44px touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .product-body .btn-secondary min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Product card buttons have a min-height of 28px, below the recommended 44px touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .hero-ctas .btn-primary min-height 32px
issuesFound.explanation: Hero CTA buttons have a min-height of 32px, below the recommended 44px touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .hero-ctas .btn-secondary min-height 32px
issuesFound.explanation: Hero secondary buttons have a min-height of 32px, below the recommended 44px touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] width/height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: Checkboxes are sized at 20x20px, below the 24px minimum requirement.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .social-links a min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Social links have a min-height of 28px, below the recommended 44px touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transform: translateY(-200%) with a transition, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 475 is empty and does not disable this motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 475: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class uses transition: transform, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block does not disable this transform motion for buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 475: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image uses transform: rotateY with a transition. The media query at line 890 exists but is empty, failing to disable the motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 890: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: Hero buttons use transition: transform. The global reduced-motion media query does not override this specific transform motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 475: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word-spacing reduces space between words, impairing readability for users with cognitive disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .section-header p foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff
issuesFound.explanation: The paragraph text uses #aaaaaa on a white background (#ffffff). The contrast ratio is approximately 2.85:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .product-body > p foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff
issuesFound.explanation: Product description text uses #aaaaaa on white. Contrast is ~2.85:1, failing WCAG AA requirements.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .pricing-section .section-header p foreground #aaaaaa / background #f8fafc
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing section description text uses #aaaaaa on #f8fafc. Contrast is below 4.5:1.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .contact-section > p foreground #aaaaaa / background #f8fafc
issuesFound.explanation: Contact section description text uses #aaaaaa on #f8fafc. Contrast is below 4.5:1.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .input-hint foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff
issuesFound.explanation: Input hint text uses #aaaaaa on white. Contrast is ~2.85:1, making it difficult to read.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .logo-bar-label foreground #94a3b8 / background #f8fafc
issuesFound.explanation: Logo bar label uses #94a3b8 on #f8fafc. Contrast is approximately 3.3:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .footer-brand > p foreground #94a3b8 / background #555555
issuesFound.explanation: Footer brand text uses #94a3b8 on #555555. Contrast is approximately 2.6:1, failing requirements.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a foreground #94a3b8 / background #555555
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links use #94a3b8 on #555555. Contrast is ~2.6:1, making links hard to distinguish.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1500: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { color: var(--colour-neutral-300); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .footer-bottom > p foreground #64748b / background #555555
issuesFound.explanation: Footer bottom text uses #64748b on #555555. Contrast is approximately 1.3:1, which is extremely low.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .social-links a foreground #94a3b8 / background #555555
issuesFound.explanation: Social links use #94a3b8 on #555555. Contrast is ~2.6:1, failing requirements.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links: #nav-menu > li > a
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links have text-decoration: none. Hover state changes color/background but does not add an underline or other non-colour indicator.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links: [id$="-submenu"] li a
issuesFound.explanation: Submenu links have text-decoration: none. Hover state does not provide an underline or non-colour differentiator.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links have text-decoration: none. Hover state only changes color, relying on colour alone to indicate interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 725: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:hover { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer nav links have text-decoration: none. Hover state only changes color, failing SC 1.4.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1500: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:hover { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links: .social-links a
issuesFound.explanation: Social links have text-decoration: none. Hover state only changes color, failing SC 1.4.1.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle functions (open, close, toggle) modify the visible state of the drawer by toggling CSS classes and the hidden attribute, but they do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (.nav-toggle). Screen reader users will not be informed whether the menu is open or closed.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav init function does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" state on the trigger button (.nav-toggle). Assistive technologies may not correctly identify the initial closed state of the menu.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav close function sets the hidden attribute and removes CSS classes, but it does not set aria-hidden="true" on the drawer element (#main-nav). The content may remain accessible to screen readers even when visually hidden.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav open and close functions do not announce the state change (e.g., "Navigation menu opened") to screen readers via a live region. Users relying on AT will not know the menu state has changed.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu _openInstance and _closeInstance functions toggle the hidden attribute and CSS classes on the panel, but they do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not know if the dropdown is open or closed.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu register function sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls but does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. The initial state is not communicated to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu panel not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu _closeInstance function sets the hidden attribute on the panel but does not set aria-hidden="true". The panel content may remain accessible to screen readers when visually hidden.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu _openInstance and _closeInstance functions do not announce the dropdown state change to screen readers via a live region. Users will not be informed when a menu opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2465: liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');
Line 2479: liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion open function sets the item state to open and removes the hidden attribute from the panel, but it does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to "false", creating an inconsistent state.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion panel not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion close function sets the hidden attribute on the answer panel but does not set aria-hidden="true". The content may remain accessible to screen readers when collapsed.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion open and close functions do not announce the state change (e.g., "Answer expanded") to screen readers via a live region. Users will not know which item was toggled.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch _open and _close functions manage the visibility of the suggestion listbox but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the search input (#search-input). Screen reader users will not know if suggestions are available.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch init function sets aria-controls and aria-haspopup on the input but does not set the initial aria-expanded="false". The initial closed state is not communicated.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch listbox not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch _close function calls suggestionListRenderer.hide() which sets the hidden attribute, but does not explicitly set aria-hidden="true" on the listbox element.

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing initial aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing init function does not set the initial aria-pressed state on the billing period buttons. The default selection is not communicated to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing setPeriod function updates the UI but does not announce the billing period change (e.g., "Prices switched to annual billing") to screen readers via a live region.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons missing initial aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter init function does not set the initial aria-pressed state on the filter tabs. The default selection is not communicated to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: productFilter results change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter applyFilters function updates the product grid and count badge but does not announce the filter change or result count to screen readers via a live region.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function adds an aria-describedby reference for the error message but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid function removes the aria-describedby reference but does not remove or reset the aria-invalid attribute on the field. The field may remain announced as invalid even when corrected.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop action not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollToTop _onClick function scrolls the page to the top but does not announce the action (e.g., "Scrolled back to top") to screen readers via a live region. Users may be disoriented by the sudden jump.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent trigger does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The cookieConsent _showBanner and _hideBanner functions manage the banner visibility but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (#cookie-settings-btn). Screen reader users will not know if the settings panel is open.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The cookieConsent init function does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" on the settings trigger button. The initial state is not communicated.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent banner state not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The cookieConsent _showBanner, _onAccept, and _onDecline functions do not announce the banner state or consent decision to screen readers via a live region.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 8344: liveRegion.announce('Cookie settings opened');
Line 8363: liveRegion.announce('Cookie preferences saved');

issuesFound.title: stickyHeader does not manage aria-hidden when visually hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The stickyHeader _onScroll function adds/removes the header--hidden class to visually hide the header, but does not set aria-hidden="true" when hidden. The header landmark may remain accessible to screen readers when not visible.

issuesFound.title: planComparisonHighlighter lacks keyboard support for hover action
issuesFound.explanation: The planComparisonHighlighter init function uses mouseenter to highlight columns in the comparison table. There is no keyboard equivalent (e.g., focus event) to trigger the highlight for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField validation does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The fileUploadField _validateFile function announces errors via liveRegion but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input or zone element when a file fails validation.

issuesFound.title: discountCodeInput validation does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The discountCodeInput _onApply function announces invalid codes via liveRegion but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input field when validation fails.

faqAccordion panel not removed from accessibility tree when closed
siteSearch listbox not removed from accessibility tree when closed

scrollToTop action not announced to screen readers

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy for loading state
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component accepts an isLoading prop but does not apply aria-busy to the button element. Screen reader users are not informed that the button is busy processing, leading to confusion about why interaction is blocked.
issuesFound.fix: Line 663: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The visual asterisk indicating a required field is not hidden from assistive technology. Screen readers may announce "asterisk" or "star" unnecessarily, cluttering the experience.

issuesFound.title: Section component ignores labelledby prop
issuesFound.explanation: The Section component accepts a labelledby prop in its interface but fails to apply it as aria-labelledby on the rendered element. This breaks the accessible naming strategy for all sections using this component.

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The primary navigation element lacks an aria-label. Since there are multiple nav elements on the page (mobile, footer), this landmark is not uniquely identifiable for screen reader navigation.

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation link indicating the current page uses a visual class but lacks aria-current="page". Screen reader users are not informed which page they are currently on.
issuesFound.fix: Line 963: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? "page" : undefined} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav close button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton used to close the mobile navigation menu does not have an aria-label. The icon content is hidden, leaving the button nameless to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1015: <IconButton onClick={onClose} aria-label="Close navigation menu" className="mobile-nav__close">

issuesFound.title: MobileNav body landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The nav element inside the mobile panel lacks an aria-label. It is indistinguishable from other navigation landmarks on the page.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The active link in the mobile navigation lacks aria-current="page". The visual indicator is not communicated to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1065: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? "page" : undefined} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={onClose}>

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle icon button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton used for theme switching does not receive an aria-label prop. The icon inside is hidden, resulting in a nameless button.

issuesFound.title: HeroSection trust badges group missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The container for trust badges and certifications is a group of related content without a heading or accessible name. Screen readers cannot identify the purpose of this group.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1295: <div className="hero-section__trust" aria-label="Trust badges and certifications">

issuesFound.title: StatsBar section missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The stats section has a visually hidden heading but the section element does not reference it via aria-labelledby. The landmark lacks an accessible name.

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar clone list missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The duplicated list used for the visual marquee effect is not hidden from assistive technology. This causes screen readers to read the integration list twice.

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter toggle buttons track active state via className but do not use aria-pressed. Screen reader users cannot identify which filter is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1630: <button type="button" aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} className={['filter-tab-btn', activeFilter === tab.value ? 'filter-tab-btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)}>

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle monthly button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly billing toggle button does not use aria-pressed to reflect its selected state. The state is only conveyed visually.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1835: <button type="button" id="billing-monthly" aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'monthly' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}>

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle annual button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The annual billing toggle button does not use aria-pressed to reflect its selected state. The state is only conveyed visually.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1842: <button type="button" id="billing-annual" aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('annual')}>

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel prev button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton for the previous slide lacks an aria-label. The chevron content is hidden, leaving the button nameless.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2115: <IconButton aria-controls={trackId} aria-label="Previous testimonial" onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev">

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton for the next slide lacks an aria-label. The chevron content is hidden, leaving the button nameless.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2121: <IconButton aria-controls={trackId} aria-label="Next testimonial" onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next">

issuesFound.title: Modal close button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton used to close the modal dialog lacks an aria-label. The icon is hidden, making the button inaccessible.

issuesFound.title: StarRating inner spans missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The individual star spans inside the labelled StarRating container are not marked as decorative. They should be hidden to prevent redundancy with the container's aria-label.
issuesFound.fix: Line 595: <span key={i} className={filled ? 'star star--filled' : 'star star--empty'} aria-hidden="true">

issuesFound.title: SiteHeader actions group missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The div containing header controls (search, theme, CTAs) is a group of related controls without an accessible name.

fpTitles: DesktopNav landmark missing accessible name
ThemeToggle icon button missing accessible name
HeroSection trust badges group missing aria-label
Modal close button missing accessible name
SiteHeader actions group missing aria-label
